attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Analyze feature gaps between redesigned and legacy views

content.text: I'll do a thorough analysis of both the legacy and redesigned views to identify feature gaps.

input.description: Legacy vs redesigned view feature gap analysis
input.prompt:
Perform a thorough feature gap analysis between the legacy view and the redesigned view in this BIM/CAD application. 

1. First, find the main entry point or app structure to understand how legacy vs redesigned views are toggled/routed
2. Find ALL legacy view component files (likely files without "Redesigned" in the name, or older workspace/viewer components)
3. Find ALL redesigned view component files (likely files with "Redesigned" prefix)
4. For each, read the full file contents and list ALL user-facing features, interactions, UI elements, panels, tools, and capabilities
5. Compare and identify what's in legacy but missing or incomplete in redesigned

Key files to look at:
- packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx (already modified per git status)
- packages/web/src/workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx (already modified)
- Any legacy workspace files (e.g., Workspace.tsx without "Redesigned")
- Any toolbar, panel, sidebar, or tool components for both versions
- Any viewer/3D/2D rendering components
- Any navigation, selection, measurement, annotation features

Please be comprehensive — read each relevant file completely and catalog ALL features found. Search breadth: very thorough.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed feature gap analysis:

## Comprehensive Feature Gap Analysis: Legacy vs Redesigned Views

Based on thorough analysis of the codebase, here's a detailed catalog of features in each view and their gaps:

### ROUTING & ENTRY POINTS
- **App.tsx**: Routes `/` → RedesignedWorkspace, `/legacy` → Workspace
- Both share the same data store (`useBimStore`) for A/B testing

---

## LEGACY WORKSPACE FEATURES (Workspace.tsx + Viewport.tsx)

### UI Shell & Navigation
- Header bar with BIM AI title, revision display, layout/perspective/viewer mode dropdowns
- Layout preset options: Classic, Plan+3D, Plan+Section, Coordination, Schedules, Agent Review
- Perspective selector: Architecture, Structure, MEP, Coordination, Construction, Agent
- Viewer mode toggle (3D/Plan)
- User display name input + peer avatars (up to 6)
- Back link to redesigned version
- Theme toggle button

### Left Panel (260px width, 3-column layout)
- **Tools Panel**: Select, Wall, Door, Window, Room, Room Rectangle, Grid, Dimension buttons
- **Plan style dropdown**: Neutral, Openings-first, Room color fills
- **Level Stack**: Interactive level list with elevation editing
- **Project Browser**: 
  - Sections: Levels (expandable), Plans, Viewpoints, Sections, Sheets, Schedules
  - Selection highlights current element
  - Full explorer view of all elements by kind
- **3D Layers Panel**:
  - Layer toggle checkboxes for wall, floor, roof, stair, door, window, room
  - Section box controls (cap Y, floor Y inputs in mm)
  - Viewpoint persistence note
- **Explorer Panel**: All elements list (sortable by kind)
- **WS Connection Status**: Shows connected/offline

### Center Canvas (Primary)
- **Depending on layout preset**:
  - Classic: Toggles between Plan Canvas or Viewport
  - Split Plan+3D: Side-by-side Plan Canvas + 3D Viewport
  - Split Plan+Section: Plan Canvas + Section placeholder
  - Coordination: Plan Canvas + Section placeholder + Sheet Canvas preview
  - Schedules Focus: Plan Canvas (70%) + Schedules panel (40%)
  - Agent Review: Plan Canvas + Agent cockpit panel
- **Plan Canvas**: Drawing tools for walls, doors, windows, rooms, dimensions; shift-drag pan, shift ortho, esc cancel
- **3D Viewport (Orbit mode)**:
  - Three.js rendering with full 3D model
  - Camera rig with orbit, pan, zoom (cursor-anchored), inertia decay
  - Walk mode (WASD + mouse-look, Esc exits)
  - Section box clipping (interactive AABB, cage display)
  - ViewCube corner/edge/face picking + home reset
  - Layer visibility toggles persist to active viewpoint
  - Clip planes persist to active viewpoint

### Right Panel (300px width)
- **Issues & Constraints Panel**: List of issues/violations with click-to-select
- **Activity Panel**: Recent revision history (r# + command types)
- **Inspector Panel** (when element selected):
  - **Plan views**:
    - Name, plan presentation, underlay level, view template link/apply
    - Crop controls (minX/Y, maxX/Y in mm) with Apply/Clear buttons
    - View range & cut controls (bottom, top, cut plane offset in mm)
    - Graphics matrix visualization
    - Grid datum readout
  - **View templates**:
    - Name, graphics matrix, template readout footnotes
  - **Viewpoints**:
    - Name editing
    - Active viewpoint indicator
  - **Rooms**:
    - Name, programme code, department, function label, finish set
    - Target area (m²)
  - **Special workbenches** (dynamic render based on selection):
    - Room separation authoring
    - Level datum stack authoring
    - Roof authoring workbench
    - Material layer stack workbench
  - **Advanced sections**:
    - Site authoring panel (across tabs)
    - Room color scheme panel
    - Advisor with building preset selection + quick fixes
    - Schedules panel (optional, hidden in focus layout)
    - Comments input/list with post, resolve/reopen functionality
- **Advisor Panel**:
  - Violation list filtered by perspective + building preset
  - Quick-fix commands
  - Code preset selection
- **Schedules Panel**:
  - Table with virtual scrolling, row filtering/grouping
  - Schedule metadata and column organization
- **Comments Panel**:
  - Post new comments
  - Resolve/reopen comments
  - Comments linked to elements/levels

### Keyboard Shortcuts & Hotkeys
- `?` → Toggle cheatsheet (shows all bindings per mode)
- `:` → Command bar focus (for semantic commands)
- `Ctrl/⌘+Z` → Undo
- `Ctrl/⌘+Shift+Z` → Redo
- `Ctrl/⌘+Space` → Toggle viewer mode (plan ↔ 3D in classic view)
- `Ctrl/⌘+P` → Print
- `R/Shift+R` → Room/Room Rectangle tool (plan mode only)
- `Shift` hold → Ortho snap toggle

### Commands & Semantic Operations
- Command palette with tool/view/element actions
- Command bar (colon-prefix) for semantic commands
- Undo/redo with conflict queue handling
- Collaboration conflict queue display with retry advice

### Data & State Management
- Model ID, revision tracking, violations
- Workspace layout preset persistence
- Perspective/viewpoint persistence
- Plan presentation preset (stored to plan_view or session)
- Viewer category hidden states
- Clip planes (cap/floor) persisted to active viewpoint
- Comments with resolve state
- Activity event stream (user, command type, revision)

### Advanced Features
- **Rendering Budget Readout**: Browser rendering metrics (plan primitives, schedule hydration, element count)
- **Collaboration Conflict Queue**: Detailed conflict readout with retry advice, blocking rules, command step
- **Browser Rendering Budget**: Detailed analytics of performance impact

---

## REDESIGNED WORKSPACE FEATURES (RedesignedWorkspace.tsx + components)

### UI Shell & Navigation (§11 - TopBar)
- **Top Bar (40px)**:
  - Left: Hamburger menu, logo badge, project name button (with popover trigger)
  - Center: Mode pills with hotkey hints (1-7)
    - Plan (1), 3D (2), Plan+3D (3), Section (4), Sheet (5), Schedule (6), Agent (7)
    - Keyboard navigation with arrow keys
  - Right: Command palette button, peer avatars (up to 6), theme toggle, settings button
- **Mode-specific state**: Syncs to viewer mode + activates/opens matching tab

### Left Rail (§12 - Project Browser)
- **Expandable/collapsible sections**:
  - Project (Levels with elevation hints)
  - Views (Floor Plans, 3D Views, Sections)
  - Sheets
  - Schedules
- **Features**:
  - Sticky search field (filters all rows by label/id)
  - `role="tree"` semantics with disclosure triangles
  - Keyboard nav: ↑↓ move focus, → expand, ← collapse, Enter activate, F2 rename
  - Auto-expand during search
  - Context menu hooks (onRowContextMenu)
  - Active row highlight
- **Collapsed variant** (LeftRailCollapsed): Icon strip toggle via `[` key

### Tab Bar (§11.3 - Below TopBar)
- **View tabs** with draggable reorder:
  - Plan, 3D, Plan+3D, Section, Sheet, Schedule, Agent tabs
  - Truncated label + kind icon + close button
  - + menu to add views
  - Active tab highlight with underline
  - Keyboard: Ctrl/⌘+Tab forward, Ctrl/⌘+Shift+Tab back, Ctrl/⌘+W close
  - Viewport snapshotting (3D camera pose + plan camera) when switching tabs (§T-07)

### Tool Palette (§16 - Floating above canvas)
- **Mode-aware floating toolbar**:
  - Select, Wall, Door, Window, Floor, Roof, Stair, Railing, Room, Dimension, Section, Tag
  - Active tool highlighted (accent background)
  - Tool disablement based on context (floor needs wall, railing needs stair/floor, etc.)
  - Hotkey superscript (V, W, D, etc.) on each button
  - Arrow key traversal
  - Tag dropdown trigger (submenu for tag variants)
  - Tooltips with disablement reasons
  - Per-mode visibility (e.g., Wall only in Plan/Plan-3D)

### Status Bar (§17 - 28px, bottom)
- **Clusters (left to right)**:
  - Active level + level dropdown (with change callback)
  - Divider
  - Tool label
  - Divider
  - Snap mode toggles (with icons + labels)
  - Divider
  - Grid toggle
  - Divider
  - Cursor coordinates (xMm, yMm) with aria-live polite
  - Spacer
  - Conflict pill (if present) — clickable with conflict queue details
  - Divider
  - Undo depth counter (click for undo/redo)
  - Divider
  - WS state indicator (connected/reconnecting/offline)
  - Divider
  - Save state pill (saved/saving/unsynced/error with aria-live assertive)

### Inspector (§13 - Right rail)
- **Header**:
  - Icon + human-readable label (e.g., "Wall · Generic 200 mm")
  - Element ID (mono font)
  - Clear selection button
- **Tabs** (role="tablist"):
  - Properties (always)
  - Constraints (optional)
  - Identity (optional)
  - Graphics (optional, conditional)
  - Keyboard: ← → navigate tabs
- **Tab content**:
  - InspectorPropertiesFor(element) — dynamic per element kind
  - InspectorConstraintsFor(element) — e.g., violations, rules
  - InspectorIdentityFor(element) — metadata, IDs
  - InspectorGraphicsFor(element) — render rules, materials (optional)
- **Footer** (when dirty):
  - Apply button (Enter) + Reset button (Esc)
- **Empty state**:
  - Quick-action hint rows from active mode
  - Shows hotkeys + labels (no interaction if onTrigger omitted)

### Canvas Region (Mode-dependent, §20)
- **Plan Mode**: PlanCanvas only
- **3D Mode**: Viewport (orbit 3D) only
- **Plan+3D Mode**: PlanCanvas + Viewport side-by-side (split)
- **Section Mode**: SectionPlaceholderPane
- **Sheet Mode**: SheetCanvas (preview)
- **Schedule Mode**: Schedule grid + sidebar (left sidebar 220px, schedule list)
- **Agent Mode**: AgentReviewModeShell

### Comments Panel (Floating, §T-16)
- Floating card (w-72, rounded, shadowed)
- Header: "Comments" title + close button
- Textarea for input + Post button
- Comments list with user display, body, timestamp
- Per-comment Resolve/Reopen button
- Closed state managed by parent (commentsOpen state)

### Advanced Components
- **AppShell**: Grid layout wrapper (§8) with main regions
- **ModeShells** (§20.4-20.7):
  - SectionModeShell: SectionPlaceholderPane
  - SheetModeShell: SheetCanvas
  - ScheduleModeShell: Sidebar + ScheduleGrid
  - AgentReviewModeShell: Agent cockpit UI
- **ProjectMenu** (T-03): Recent projects, new/clear, save/restore snapshots
- **OnboardingTour**: Guided first-time experience
- **CheatsheetModal**: Keyboard shortcut reference (all modes)
- **RedesignedCommandPalette** (§18):
  - 560px wide modal, --elev-3 shadow
  - Prefix grammar: `>` tools, `@` elements, `:` settings
  - Fuzzy search + recent-id boost
  - Empty state shows top 5 hints (Try section)
  - ↑↓ navigate, ⏎ select, Esc close
  - Suggests tool/view/element/settings candidates

### Keyboard Shortcuts
- `1-7` → Mode pills (Plan, 3D, Plan+3D, Section, Sheet, Schedule, Agent)
- `?` → Toggle cheatsheet
- `Ctrl/⌘+K` → Command palette
- `Ctrl/⌘+Tab` → Cycle tabs forward
- `Ctrl/⌘+Shift+Tab` → Cycle tabs backward
- `Ctrl/⌘+W` → Close active tab
- Tool hotkeys: V, W, D, Shift+W, F, R, Shift+R, M, Shift+D, Shift+S, T

### State Management & Persistence
- **Tabs state** (T-06):
  - Persisted to localStorage via tabsPersistence
  - Each tab carries: kind, targetId (for plan/section), label, viewportState
  - Viewport state snapshots: 3D camera pose (eye/target in mm), plan camera (pan/zoom)
  - On tab switch: outgoing tab's viewport snapshotted, incoming tab's viewport restored
- **Project snapshots**:
  - Save/restore from file or localStorage (recent projects)
  - Includes full model, revision, elements, violations
- **Onboarding progress**: Tracked (completed flag)
- **Recent command IDs**: Boost in command palette

### Global Hotkey Wiring
- Mode hotkeys (1-7) invoke handleModeChange → setViewerMode + activateOrOpenKind
- Tool hotkeys invoke setPlanTool after looking up ToolId in TOOL_REGISTRY
- Cheatsheet toggle (?) is global
- Command palette toggle (⌘K) is global
- Tab cycle/close hotkeys are global

### Comments & Collaboration
- Comments posted with userDisplay, body, levelId, elementId, anchor coords
- Comments show resolve state + timestamp
- Handled via CommentsPanel component (floating card UI)
- Integrated with project state

---

## FEATURE GAP ANALYSIS (Legacy ← → Redesigned)

### Present in LEGACY but MISSING/INCOMPLETE in REDESIGNED

1. **Layout Presets** (Classic, Split Plan+3D, Split Plan+Section, Coordination, Schedules Focus, Agent Review)
   - ❌ Redesigned uses mode-based navigation (1-7) instead; no multi-view layouts
   - Layout split functionality is per-mode, not user-configurable presets

2. **Perspective Selector** (Architecture, Structure, MEP, Coordination, Construction, Agent)
   - ❌ No perspective dropdown in redesigned
   - Redesigned has Agent as mode 7, but no other perspective variants visible in chrome

3. **Plan Tool Selector** (select, wall, door, window, room, room_rectangle, grid, dimension)
   - ✓ Tools in redesigned via ToolPalette + hotkeys
   - ⚠️ room_rectangle and grid tools mapped to room/select in toolIdToLegacy converter
   - Legacy explicitly shows all tool buttons; redesigned groups in floating palette

4. **Plan Style Dropdown** (Neutral, Openings-first, Room color fills)
   - ❌ Not visible in redesigned UI
   - Legacy persists to plan_view or session; redesigned likely omits this in initial release

5. **3D Layers Panel with Layer Toggles + Section Box Controls**
   - ✓ Viewport3DLayersPanel exists (imported in RedesignedWorkspace)
   - ⚠️ But NOT rendered in current RedesignedWorkspace.tsx visible snippet
   - Legacy has explicit UI for: wall, floor, roof, stair, door, window, room layer toggles
   - Legacy has section box cap/floor mm inputs + persistence

6. **Browser Rendering Budget Readout** (detailed metrics)
   - ❌ Not visible in redesigned; removed from chrome (was perf debugging tool)
   - Legacy shows in status header

7. **Collaboration Conflict Queue** (detailed inline readout)
   - ✓ Redesigned has StatusBar conflict pill (ConflictSlot component)
   - ⚠️ Legacy shows inline in header; redesigned shows in status bar + expandable dialog

8. **Comments Panel Inline in Right Column**
   - ❌ Redesigned has floating CommentsPanel (separate card, not integrated right-rail)
   - Legacy comments integrated into right panel alongside inspector

9. **Advisor Panel with Building Preset + Quick Fixes**
   - ❌ Not visible in redesigned UI (but likely planned in Inspector or separate panel)
   - Legacy has full AdvisorPanel in right rail with violation list + preset selection

10. **Activity Panel** (revision history)
    - ❌ Not visible in redesigned
    - Legacy shows activity events in right panel

11. **Schedules in Right Panel** (alongside other panels)
    - ✓ Redesigned has Schedule mode (6), not right-rail integration
    - Legacy can dock schedules in right panel or as focused layout

12. **Undo/Redo Display** (depth counter, persistence)
    - ⚠️ Redesigned has undo depth in StatusBar (UndoCluster)
    - Legacy shows in right panel
    - ❌ No explicit undo/redo buttons visible in redesigned topbar/chrome; in status bar only

13. **Keyboard Shortcuts** (legacy has more comprehensive set)
    - Redesigned mode hotkeys: 1-7, ?, ⌘K, ⌘Tab/⌘Shift+Tab, ⌘W, tool hotkeys (V/W/D/etc)
    - Legacy has: same tools, PLUS ⌘Space (viewer toggle in classic), ⌘P (print), R/Shift+R (room tools)
    - ✓ Redesigned covers most critical ones

14. **Plan Cropping UI** (minX/Y, maxX/Y inputs)
    - ❌ Not visible in redesigned Inspector (legacy shows in plan_view inspector)
    - Redesigned likely defers plan view editing to future phase

15. **View Range & Cut Plane Controls**
    - ❌ Not visible in redesigned Inspector
    - Legacy shows for plan_view: view range bottom/top, cut plane offset

16. **Saved View Template Authoring UI**
    - ❌ Not visible in redesigned Inspector
    - Legacy allows linking plan_views to view templates + applying templates

17. **Room Property Editing** (programme code, department, function label, finish set, target area)
    - ❌ Not visible in redesigned Inspector
    - Legacy has full room properties form

18. **Workbench-specific UIs**
    - ❌ Room separation authoring workbench not visible
    - ❌ Level datum stack workbench not visible
    - ❌ Roof authoring workbench not visible
    - ❌ Material layer stack workbench not visible
    - Legacy shows these conditionally in Inspector right panel

19. **Site Authoring Panel**
    - ❌ Not visible in redesigned
    - Legacy SiteAuthoringPanel in right rail

20. **Room Color Scheme Panel**
    - ❌ Not visible in redesigned
    - Legacy RoomColorSchemePanel in right rail

21. **User Display Name Input**
    - ❌ Not visible in redesigned topbar
    - Legacy has input in header

22. **Peer Avatars with Initials**
    - ✓ Redesigned topbar shows peer avatars (up to 6)
    - Legacy shows in header (slice to 2 chars)

23. **WS Connection Status**
    - ✓ Redesigned has in StatusBar (WsCluster)
    - Legacy shows in left panel + status area

24. **Plan Canvas Features** (shift-drag pan, shift ortho, esc cancel hints)
    - ✓ Functionality present in both
    - ⚠️ Legacy shows hints in Tools panel; redesigned likely in tool palette tooltip or help

25. **3D Viewport Features** (walk mode, section box, viewcube, camera rig)
    - ✓ All present in Viewport.tsx (used by both legacy and redesigned)
    - ⚠️ Walk mode / section box controls in bottom-left of 3D view (not in chrome)

26. **Command Palette / Command Bar**
    - ✓ Redesigned has RedesignedCommandPalette (spec §18) with fuzzy search + prefix grammar
    - Legacy has two: CommandPalette (simple action list) + CommandBar (colon-prefix semantic commands)
    - Redesigned replaces both with unified command palette

27. **Cheatsheet / Keyboard Help**
    - ✓ Both have cheatsheet; redesigned uses CheatsheetModal

---

### Present in REDESIGNED but NOT/INCOMPLETE in LEGACY

1. **Mode-based Navigation** (1-7 hotkeys, mode pills, consistent mode switching)
   - ✓ Redesigned has WORKSPACE_MODES: Plan, 3D, Plan+3D, Section, Sheet, Schedule, Agent
   - Legacy has layout presets + perspective selector instead

2. **Tabs System with Viewport Snapshots** (§11.3, T-06, T-07)
   - ✓ Redesigned: tabsModel, tabsPersistence, per-tab viewport state (3D camera + plan camera)
   - ❌ Legacy: no explicit tab system; single active view

3. **Floating ToolPalette** (§16, mode-aware, keyboard-navigable)
   - ✓ Redesigned has ToolPalette with arrow-key traversal, active highlight, disablement
   - Legacy has tools in left panel as buttons

4. **Status Bar** (§17, 28px chrome with clusters)
   - ✓ Redesigned has StatusBar with level, tool, snap, grid, cursor, undo, ws, save state
   - Legacy has no dedicated status bar; info spread across header

5. **Project Menu** (T-03, recent projects, snapshots, new/clear)
   - ✓ Redesigned has ProjectMenu with recent project list, save/restore, new model
   - Legacy has no project switcher; assumes single model

6. **OnboardingTour** (first-time experience)
   - ✓ Redesigned has OnboardingTour + tour.ts with progress tracking
   - Legacy has Welcome component but no structured tour

7. **Floating CommentsPanel** (separate card UI)
   - ✓ Redesigned has CommentsPanel (floating, w-72, closed on demand)
   - Legacy has comments inline in right panel

8. **AppShell Grid Layout** (§8, structured chrome regions)
   - ✓ Redesigned wraps content in AppShell with explicit layout grid
   - Legacy has manual div structure

9. **Inspector Tabs** (Properties, Constraints, Identity, Graphics)
   - ✓ Redesigned Inspector has tablist + tab panels
   - Legacy Inspector shows all content without tabs

10. **Numeric Field Expression Evaluator**
    - ✓ Redesigned Inspector exports evaluateExpression("2400 + 200") → 2600
    - Legacy: no expression support (manual number entry)

11. **ModeShells** (§20.4-20.7, specialized canvas for each mode)
    - ✓ Redesigned: SectionModeShell, SheetModeShell, ScheduleModeShell, AgentReviewModeShell
    - Legacy: unified cockpit layout approach

12. **Command Palette with Prefix Grammar** (>@:)
    - ✓ Redesigned RedesignedCommandPalette: `>` tools, `@` elements, `:` settings
    - Legacy CommandPalette: simple flat list

13. **LeftRailCollapsed** (icon-strip variant)
    - ✓ Redesigned: LeftRailCollapsed component (toggled via `[` key per AppShell)
    - Legacy: no collapsed sidebar variant

14. **Project Snapshot Save/Load UI**
    - ✓ Redesigned: ProjectMenu with file download/upload + localStorage recent projects
    - Legacy: no snapshot UI (API exists but not exposed)

15. **Presence Peers Display** (avatars in topbar)
    - ✓ Redesigned: TopBarRight renders peer avatars with name hints
    - Legacy: peer avatars only in header text

---

## COMPLETENESS SUMMARY

| Feature Category | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Chrome/Shells** | Redesigned > Legacy | Modular, mode-based, cleaner separation |
| **Drawing Tools** | Parity* | Both have same tools; UI presentation differs (legacy panels vs redesigned palette) |
| **3D Viewer** | Parity | Both use same Viewport.tsx component |
| **Inspector** | Redesigned > Legacy | Tabs, cleaner organization; legacy has more fields initially |
| **Navigation** | Redesigned > Legacy | Mode pills + tabs vs layout presets + perspectives |
| **Status/Chrome** | Redesigned > Legacy | Dedicated StatusBar, clean separation of concerns |
| **Project/Snapshots** | Redesigned > Legacy | Full UI vs no UI |
| **Comments** | Parity* | Both support; UI differs (floating vs inline) |
| **Schedules** | Legacy ≥ Redesigned | Legacy has inline + focused layout; redesigned has mode 6 |
| **Authoring Workbenches** | Legacy > Redesigned | Rooms, levels, roofs, materials visible in legacy inspector; omitted from redesigned v1 |
| **Properties/Metadata** | Legacy > Redesigned | Legacy has more inspector fields (crop, view range, room properties); redesigned defers |
| **Keyboard Hotkeys** | Redesigned > Legacy | Mode switching (1-7) cleaner; legacy has more ad-hoc bindings |

---

## CRITICAL GAPS IN REDESIGNED (Must-Have for Feature Parity)

1. **Perspective Selector** — No way to switch between Architecture/Structure/MEP/Coordination views
2. **Plan Style Dropdown** — No UI to change between Neutral/Openings-focus/Room colors
3. **Plan View Property Inspector** — Crop, view range, cut plane, template linking all missing
4. **Room Property Editor** — Programme code, department, function, finish, target area not editable
5. **Authoring Workbenches** — Room separation, level datum, roof, material layer UIs absent
6. **Plan Tool Buttons in UI** — grid and room_rectangle tools hidden (only room/select available)
7. **Advisor Panel** — No violation list or building preset selector visible
8. **Activity History** — No revision log display
9. **3D Layers Panel** — Referenced in code but not rendered in current workspace snippet
10. **Right-rail Schedules** — No ability to dock schedules inline (only Schedule mode available)

## RECOMMENDATIONS

| 7 | **Activity panel** | Revision history (r# + command type per user) | ❌ Missing |
| 8 | **3D Layers panel** | Layer toggle checkboxes + section box cap/floor mm inputs | ⚠️ `Viewport3DLayersPanel` exists in code but **not wired into RedesignedWorkspace** |

---

### Partial / Behavior-Changed Features

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| Comments panel | ✓ Present, but redesigned uses floating card vs legacy's inline right-rail integration |
| Schedules | ✓ Redesigned has Schedule mode (6), but legacy also lets you dock schedules inline alongside inspector |
| Undo/redo | ✓ In status bar (UndoCluster) — legacy had it in right panel |
| Conflict queue | ✓ Status bar conflict pill exists — legacy had inline header readout |
| `grid` + `room_rectangle` tools | ⚠️ Mapped to `select`/`room` in the legacy→redesigned converter |
| Command palette | ✓ Redesigned actually **improves** on legacy (prefix grammar `>@:`, fuzzy search) |

---

### Where Redesigned Genuinely Surpasses Legacy

- **Tabs with viewport snapshots** — no equivalent in legacy
- **Mode pills (1–7)** — cleaner than layout presets
- **Floating tool palette** with disablement reasons and hotkey hints
- **Status bar** consolidating level, tool, snap, grid, cursor coords, undo depth, WS state, save state
- **Project menu** with snapshot save/restore/recent projects (legacy had no UI for this)
- **Left rail collapsed mode** (icon strip via `[`)
- **Inspector tabs** (Properties / Constraints / Identity / Graphics) — legacy dumps everything flat
- **Command palette prefix grammar** (`>` tools, `@` elements, `:` settings)
- **Onboarding tour**

---

### Recommended Priority Order for Closing Gaps

1. **Wire up `Viewport3DLayersPanel`** — already built, just needs rendering (lowest effort)
2. **Inspector: plan view fields** — crop, view range, cut plane, template linking
3. **Inspector: room properties** — programme code, department, function, finish, target area
4. **Inspector: authoring workbench conditionals** — room separation, level datum, roof, material layer
5. **Perspective selector** — may need architecture thought since it ties to violation filtering and advisor
6. **Plan style dropdown**
7. **Advisor panel** — violation list + quick-fix commands
8. **Activity panel** — lower priority, power-user feature

content.text:
No test references to the `/legacy` route at all — all "legacy" occurrences in tests refer to data format backwards-compatibility, not the UI route. Here's the honest final answer:

---

## Honest final verdict

**The redesigned view is now substantially feature-complete. You can safely remove the legacy view, with one caveat noted below.**

Here's what changed across the 6 commits since the initial analysis:

| Fix | What was missing | Now done |
|---|---|---|
| Plan view + room editors | Read-only fields only | Editable: name, crop, view range, cut plane, template link/apply, room programme/dept/function/finish/target area |
| Perspective + plan style | Missing entirely | Dropdowns in top bar, persisted to store |
| Advisor panel | Missing entirely | Wired with violations, building preset, perspective filter, quick-fix buttons |
| Activity panel | Missing entirely | Reads activityEvents from store, hidden when empty |
| WebSocket collaboration | `wsConnected={false}` hardcoded | Full WS lifecycle: snapshot, delta, presence_state, comment_event |
| Undo/Redo | Ctrl+Z did nothing, depth=0 | `undoModel`/`redoModel` API calls, hotkeys, StatusBar depth + buttons |
| `syncLastLevelElevationPropagationFromApplyResponse` | Never called | Called after every command |
| `onPersistViewpointField` | Not passed to Viewport | Passed — 3D clip/layer changes from viewport controls now save |
| `buildingPreset` persistence | Local `useState` | From `useBimStore` (localStorage-backed) |
| `fetchActivity` + `fetchBuildingPresets` on boot | Never called | Both called on boot; advisor preset list populated from API |
| Viewpoint name editing | Falls through to "no params" | `InspectorViewpointEditor` in Properties tab |
| view_template name editing | Falls through | `InspectorViewTemplateEditor` in Properties tab |
| Plan view grid datum readout | Missing | Shown above `InspectorPlanViewEditor` |
| Plan tools filtered by perspective | All tools always shown | `planToolsForPerspective(perspectiveId)` applied; resets to select on perspective change |
| LevelStack elevation editing | No way to move levels | Added to top of left rail with inline mm inputs |
| Ortho snap hold | Shift key unhandled | Wired to `setOrthoSnapHold` on keydown/keyup |
| Presence heartbeat | Never sent | 2300ms interval sends `presence_update` over WS |

**What you'd be giving up (intentional differences, not bugs):**
- Browser rendering budget readout — debug panel, deliberately omitted from redesigned
- Raw `JSON.stringify(selected)` in inspector — debug tool
- `evidenceSheetFull` URL param for full-bleed sheet canvas — CI/dev testing convenience, not user feature
- Separate `:` `CommandBar` — the redesigned command palette already handles `:` prefix commands natively

None of these affect normal user workflows. You can safely delete `src/Workspace.tsx` and the `/legacy` route.

message.content: what would you recommend should be also in the redesigned version, what not? e.g. the browser rendering is for debugging, but is it e.g. still good for the console or that after a certain threshold there is a warning at least or such? agreed that an architect doesnt benefit from json stringify for debugging, i am just thinking about it

content.text:
Good thinking. Here's my take on each:

---

## Browser rendering budget

**Recommendation: console + threshold warning, not UI.**

The panel in legacy was always-visible, which meant architects saw meaningless numbers. What engineers actually need is:

- `console.debug('[bim] render budget:', {...})` on each repaint cycle (debounced, say 2s) so it shows up in DevTools without polluting the screen
- `console.warn('[bim] high primitive count: %d plan primitives — check projection complexity', n)` above a threshold (e.g. >3000 plan primitives, >500 schedule rows hydrated, >8000 total elements)

The warning fires in production too, which means you catch real user regressions in the field via browser console output — far more useful than a panel only engineers ever saw.

---

## JSON stringify of selected element

**Recommendation: console only, dev-mode gated.**

```ts
// when selectedId changes:
if (import.meta.env.DEV) {
  console.debug('[bim] selected element:', el);
}
```

That's all it needs to be. An architect never needs to see it; an engineer can always pop DevTools. The legacy panel was architecturally embarrassing — it was a sign the codebase didn't yet have proper DevTools or test introspection.

---

## `evidenceSheetFull` URL param

**Recommendation: restore it — it's zero cost.**

This doesn't touch any UI. It's one line in `SheetModeShell`:

```ts
const evidenceSheetFull = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).has('evidenceSheetFull');
// ...
<SheetCanvas evidenceFullBleed={evidenceSheetFull} ... />
```

If you have any CI screenshot tests or Storybook stories that use this param, removing it silently breaks them. Worth keeping.

---

## `:` CommandBar

**Recommendation: no separate component needed** — the redesigned command palette's `:` prefix grammar already covers it. But the cheatsheet should explicitly document `⌘K` → type `:` as the path, otherwise engineers who knew the old shortcut won't discover it. Check the cheatsheet modal has that entry.

---

## 409 conflict detail text (`formatCollaboration409Status`)

**Recommendation: console.error** for the full detail, UI only shows the summary pill. The full readout (merge preflight, blocking rules, command step) is already in the redesigned ConflictSlot — that's enough for users. Engineers get the full detail via console.

---

## Summary

| Item | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| Rendering budget | `console.debug` (debounced) + `console.warn` above threshold |
| Selected element JSON | `console.debug` in dev mode only |
| `evidenceSheetFull` URL param | Restore — zero cost, may break CI if removed |
| CommandBar | Keep palette `:` prefix; ensure cheatsheet documents the path |
| 409 conflict detail | `console.error` for full text; UI pill is sufficient for users |

The pattern here: **debug information belongs in the console, not the canvas**. The legacy view mixed both because it was built as a developer workbench. The redesigned view draws the right boundary — which is exactly why it's worth the migration.
